element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundational mechanic used throughout entire game; represents shift from turn-based to real-time combat ordering

element_introduction_order.element: Party composition and character class system
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Game opening with Cecil Harvey (Dark Knight)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Each character has unchangeable class; party composition changes narratively throughout story. Up to 5-character parties (first in series)
Players navigate towers, caves, forests, and towns. Interact with NPCs to gather clues and advance narrative
Swords, armor, and items purchased in towns or found in dungeons increase damage output and defensive capability
Sleep at inns, use potions, or cast healing magic to restore HP. Resource scarcity creates strategic tension
Random intervals on overworld and in dungeons create unpredictable tension cycles
Front-line vs back-line placement affects damage dealt and received. Tactical depth increases with party complexity
Certain characters have unique abilities beyond standard Attack/Magic/Item/Defend options
Unlike earlier FF games, selected party members evolve their vocation to suit narrative requirements, forcing strategic adaptation
Golbez's quest to seize crystals and destroy world. Escalates tension toward late game

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching towns for healing and equipment purchases
Story progression dialogue and character development
typical_tension_triggers: Random monster encounters during overworld travel
Entering new dungeons with unknown layouts
Resource depletion (low HP, limited healing items)
Narrative story beats involving world-threatening events
Forced party composition changes via character loss/evolution
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Alternating cycle of exploration tension (random encounters, resource uncertainty) relieved by town safe havens and story progression beats. Escalating narrative stakes with Golbez conflict drive macro tension curve toward climax

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-paced introduction to ATB system, basic equipment, single character (Cecil). Safe learning environment with moderate random encounters
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Party composition expands to 5 characters. Equipment variety increases. Forced class changes introduce tactical adaptation pressure. Narrative stakes escalate with Golbez threat intensification
difficulty_curve.late_game: Complex party synergies required. Boss encounters present significant challenge. Crystal acquisition races create temporal pressure. World-threatening consequences shape final encounters
notable_spikes: First major boss encounter teaching ATB system urgency
Forced character loss or class evolution events creating tactical disruption
Golbez confrontations raising narrative and mechanical stakes
Final crystal-related encounters with world-ending consequences

notable_level_patterns.description: Settlements function as tension relief zones. Players restore HP/MP, purchase equipment, gather narrative exposition, and prepare for next dungeon push. Strategic shopping decisions add light resource management layer
Multi-floor structures with random encounters, equipment treasure, and boss culminations. Spatial navigation and resource depletion create sustained tension until reaching town or checkpoint
Open field map space between locations. Random encounter pressure, visual direction toward objective, and freedom to explore optional areas. Tension maintained by unpredictable combat
Single, significant enemy or enemy group. Demands accumulated resources, full party synergy, and tactical positioning knowledge. Story-significant, creates major tension/relief cycle peak
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Golbez encounters and major narrative bosses

notable_level_patterns.description: Story events mandate party composition changes or character class evolution. Disrupts established tactical patterns, forces player adaptation, adds dramatic stakes to mechanical system
